item.title: Salesforce Touts AI Promise Over Reality in SaaSpocalypse Fight
item.description: Patients at the University of Chicago Medicine featured in a promotional video seamlessly refill prescriptions, book appointments and even get parking tips with the help of Agentforce, Salesforce Inc.’s flagship AI tool.

item.title: China Cracks Down on Illegal Cross-Border Securities Trading
item.description: China is launching a sweeping campaign against illicit cross-border securities, futures and fund trading, in an unprecedented crackdown that aims to stamp out such activities within two years.

item.title: ECB Hike Likely in June to Signal Commitment to 2%, Demarco Says
item.description: The European Central Bank will likely raise interest rates next month to underline its commitment to deliver on its mandate, according to Governing Council member Alexander Demarco.

item.title: AI Cartoon ‘Critterz’ Misses Cannes Debut After OpenAI Shut Sora
item.description: Critterz, a feature-length cartoon intended to showcase how OpenAI’s video-generation capabilities could revolutionize filmmaking, has missed its planned Cannes Film Festival debut after the artificial intelligence company shut down its Sora tool, forcing its creators to look for a new AI partner.

item.title: Mol Shares Drop After Petrochemicals Plant Explosion in Hungary
item.description: Shares in Mol Nyrt., Hungary’s biggest energy group, dropped after an explosion at its petrochemicals plant in eastern Hungary killed one person and seriously injured several others.

item.title: Kenyan President Rules Out Further Fuel-Tax Cuts After Protests
item.description: Kenyan President William Ruto ruled out further cuts to fuel taxes, even after deadly protests over the high cost of diesel brought the nation’s capital to a standstill this week.

item.title: Total Explores Selling Stakes in European Green Assets
item.description: TotalEnergies SE is exploring selling a 50% stake in some of its European renewables assets, as the French energy giant seeks a partner for its green investments, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: ‘The Assassin’ Fahmi Quadir on How to Survive as a Short-Seller
item.description: Plus, a conversation about the Korean stock market

item.title: Short-Seller Dubbed ‘The Assassin’ Is Going Long Korea Stocks
item.description: One of the most prominent names among a shrinking group of short-sellers who make their money by betting against stocks has a new strategy: going long the world’s best-performing market.

item.title: Odd Lots: Fahmi Quadir on Surviving as a Short-Seller (Podcast)
item.description: A short-seller is a gum shoe who roots out a particular story about a specific company and brings it to light. In this way, a short-seller is similar to an investigative journalist. Fahmi Quadir, the founder and CIO of Safkhet Capital, is known as the Assassin. Her notoriety proceeds her: Among her most well-known bets include short-selling Wirecard AG and Valeant. In today’s conversation with Qaudir — recorded at our live show in London at Wilton’s Music Hall — she dishes on what life is like f

item.title: StanChart CEO Apologizes for ‘Lower-Value Human’ Comments
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc Chief Executive Officer Bill Winters apologized for his comments earlier this week about how artificial intelligence will affect “lower-value human capital,” after such remarks spurred public backlash.

item.title: China Restricts Chemical Exports to US in Fentanyl Crackdown
item.description: China imposed new export controls on some key chemical ingredients shipped to the US, Mexico and Canada, in a further sign of cooperation with Washington on curbing drug trafficking.

item.title: Sri Lankan Rupee Rallies Most in Three Years, Leads Asia FX
item.description: The Sri Lankan rupee surged against the dollar, snapping a nine-day losing streak to become Asia’s best-performing currency on Friday.

item.title: EU-Anthropic Talks Over Mythos Concerns Are Stalled, Spain Says
item.description: The European Union has made little progress in talks with Anthropic PBC to get its banks and companies tested for digital vulnerabilities that the new Mythos AI model uncovers, Spain’s economy minister said.

item.title: China Scrutinizes Companies, Funds After AI-Fueled Stock Moves
item.description: Chinese regulators are scrutinizing recent stock rallies that have been fueled by artificial intelligence optimism, asking some listed companies and funds to give more details about their approach to the technology, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Qatar Airways to Skip Staff Bonuses Due to Iran War Impact
item.description: Qatar Airways QCSC will skip handing out bonuses to almost 60,000 workers this year after the US-Israel war on Iran forced the carrier to cancel tens of thousands of flights and lose out on billions in revenue.

item.title: South Korea Says Won Moves Excessive, Warns of Market Actions
item.description: South Korea’s finance ministry and the central bank said they were closely monitoring the dollar-won exchange rate and warned they would “take decisive actions if necessary.”

item.title: Palm Oil Markets Split as Indonesia Export Revamp Confuses Trade
item.description: Palm oil buyers are picking up spot cargoes at bargain rates even as futures prices climb, as traders attempt to understand the impact of a radical export overhaul announced by top producer Indonesia earlier this week.

item.title: French Business Confidence Languishes Below Long-Term Average
item.description: French business confidence remained weak in May, adding to evidence that the fallout from the Iran war is weighing on domestic demand in the euro area’s second-largest economy.

item.title: Japan to Receive First Oil Tanker to Exit Hormuz Since War Began
item.description: Japan — one of Asia’s biggest importers of energy from the Middle East — flagged the impending arrival of the first Persian Gulf oil shipment to transit the contested Strait of Hormuz since the Iran war began.

item.title: GBL Backs CVC Offer to Buy Italy’s Recordati for $12 Billion
item.description: Groupe Bruxelles Lambert agreed to back CVC Capital Partners Plc’s offer to buy Recordati SpA in a deal valuing the Italian drugmaker at more than $12 billion, which would be one of the biggest healthcare transactions in Europe this year.

item.title: Germany’s Exporters Helped Drive First-Quarter Growth Spurt
item.description: Germany’s export machine showed signs of life at the start of the year, combining with government spending to drive a spurt in growth before the Iran war hit.

item.title: Strategist Warnings of War Impact on European Stocks Grow Louder
item.description: Unless the Strait of Hormuz reopens soon, the case for European stocks will degrade materially, according to strategists surveyed by Bloomberg.

item.title: Chinese Brands Snap Up Record 15% of Europe’s Electric-Car Sales
item.description: Chinese automakers accounted for more than 15% of electric-vehicle sales in Europe for the first time last month as consumer appetite for models from the country shows no sign of slowing.

item.title: UK Retail Sales Plunge as Iran War Leads to Fewer Car Trips
item.description: UK retail sales fell at the fastest pace in nearly a year as consumers pulled back spending and made fewer car journeys amid the global energy shock from war in Iran.

item.title: UK Posts Highest April Budget Deficit Since the Pandemic
item.description: UK government borrowing hit the highest level for any April in six years, as pressure on the public finances mounts from the Iran war and political instability at home.

item.title: EU’s Dombrovskis Sees ECB Rate Response to Inflation Shock
item.description: European Union economy chief Valdis Dombrovskis said the European Central Bank will need to address the continent’s rising inflation due to the war in Iran.

item.title: UK Bond Yields Set for Biggest Weekly Decline Since 2024
Richemont Sales Climb on Resilient Demand for Jewelry
item.description: Richemont’s full-year sales rose more than expected as shoppers splurged on its pricey Cartier bracelets and rings, helping the Swiss group weather a luxury market slowdown better than most rivals.

item.title: Turkey Looks to Calm Markets After Court Blow to Opposition
item.description: Turkey’s top economic policymakers will meet on Friday morning to discuss measures to stem market turbulence after a court ordered the removal of the main opposition party’s leadership.
